visa_types.name: Business/ Conference
visa_types.documents_required:
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.
Please refer to the attached recognized list of Travel Medical Insurance Companies. 
The insured party, policy validity period, geographical coverage and schedule of benefits must be mentioned on the policy schedule/certificate.
Applicants are required to submit colored copy of passport and ID which shows clearly all information.
Translation of all Arabic documents into English or Slovak is mandatory.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[svk > sau > en > Table:Business/Conference > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 156.40 SAR (Inclusive of 15% VAT) per application.
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in Saudi Riyals as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

[svk > sau > en > Table: > Visa Fees 2]
visa_types.processing_time: From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.
visa_types.forms:
Download Application Form
This form must be completed when you are applying for a visa as a family
This form needs to be filled only when you are applying the visa for minor 
List of travel medical insurance companies – Click here  
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for business purposes. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days. This visa will be issued for business purpose only; for gainful professional activities (even if the work is not financially rewarded) you have to apply for a work permit.
It is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for a foreigner who is also a citizen of the Slovak Republic
The Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh informs that it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for Slovak citizens in a travel document issued by a third country. Such visa application is not admissible.
If the child's parent is demonstrably a citizen of the Slovak Republic, it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for the child in a travel document issued by a third country.
A citizen of the Slovak Republic shall request the issuance of a passport or the issuance of a emergency travel document in order to travel to Slovak Republic. The emergency travel document is valid only for travel to Slovakia.
Please find more information at: Schengen Visa | Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Business visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for business purposes. This visa is given for travel period of less than 90 days. This visa will be issued for business purpose only; for gainful professional activities (even if the work is not financially rewarded) you have to apply for a work permit.
It is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for a foreigner who is also a citizen of the Slovak Republic
The Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh informs that it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for Slovak citizens in a travel document issued by a third country. Such visa application is not admissible.
If the child's parent is demonstrably a citizen of the Slovak Republic, it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for the child in a travel document issued by a third country.
A citizen of the Slovak Republic shall request the issuance of a passport or the issuance of a emergency travel document in order to travel to Slovak Republic. The emergency travel document is valid only for travel to Slovakia.
Please find more information at: Schengen Visa | Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh 

## Visa Fees

[svk > sau > en > Table:Business/Conference > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 156.40 SAR (Inclusive of 15% VAT) per application.
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in Saudi Riyals as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

[svk > sau > en > Table: > Visa Fees 2]

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.
Please refer to the attached recognized list of Travel Medical Insurance Companies. 
The insured party, policy validity period, geographical coverage and schedule of benefits must be mentioned on the policy schedule/certificate.
Applicants are required to submit colored copy of passport and ID which shows clearly all information.
Translation of all Arabic documents into English or Slovak is mandatory.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

## Download Forms
Download Application Form
This form must be completed when you are applying for a visa as a family
This form needs to be filled only when you are applying the visa for minor 
List of travel medical insurance companies – Click here  
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Slovakia for Tourist purposes. Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
It is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for a foreigner who is also a citizen of the Slovak Republic
The Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh informs that it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for Slovak citizens in a travel document issued by a third country. Such visa application is not admissible.
If the child's parent is demonstrably a citizen of the Slovak Republic, it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for the child in a travel document issued by a third country.
A citizen of the Slovak Republic shall request the issuance of a passport or the issuance of a emergency travel document in order to travel to Slovak Republic. The emergency travel document is valid only for travel to Slovakia.
Please find more information at: Schengen Visa | Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Slovakia for Tourist purposes. Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
It is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for a foreigner who is also a citizen of the Slovak Republic
The Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh informs that it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for Slovak citizens in a travel document issued by a third country. Such visa application is not admissible.
If the child's parent is demonstrably a citizen of the Slovak Republic, it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for the child in a travel document issued by a third country.
A citizen of the Slovak Republic shall request the issuance of a passport or the issuance of a emergency travel document in order to travel to Slovak Republic. The emergency travel document is valid only for travel to Slovakia.
Please find more information at: Schengen Visa | Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh 

## Visa Fees

[svk > sau > en > Table:Medical Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 156.40 SAR (Inclusive of 15% VAT) per application.
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in Saudi Riyals as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
This is an optional Value-added Service Available:

[svk > sau > en > Table: > Visa Fees 2]

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.
Please refer to the attached recognized list of Travel Medical Insurance Companies. 
The insured party, policy validity period, geographical coverage and schedule of benefits must be mentioned on the policy schedule/certificate.
Applicants are required to submit colored copy of passport and ID which shows clearly all information.
Translation of all Arabic documents into English or Slovak is mandatory.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background

- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre.

From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Slovakia for study, research, or internship purposes (for less than 90 days). Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
It is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for a foreigner who is also a citizen of the Slovak Republic
The Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh informs that it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for Slovak citizens in a travel document issued by a third country. Such visa application is not admissible.
If the child's parent is demonstrably a citizen of the Slovak Republic, it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for the child in a travel document issued by a third country.
A citizen of the Slovak Republic shall request the issuance of a passport or the issuance of a emergency travel document in order to travel to Slovak Republic. The emergency travel document is valid only for travel to Slovakia.
Please find more information at: Schengen Visa | Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh

## Overview
This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Slovakia for study, research, or internship purposes (for less than 90 days). Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
It is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for a foreigner who is also a citizen of the Slovak Republic
The Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh informs that it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for Slovak citizens in a travel document issued by a third country. Such visa application is not admissible.
If the child's parent is demonstrably a citizen of the Slovak Republic, it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for the child in a travel document issued by a third country.
A citizen of the Slovak Republic shall request the issuance of a passport or the issuance of a emergency travel document in order to travel to Slovak Republic. The emergency travel document is valid only for travel to Slovakia.
Please find more information at: Schengen Visa | Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For guidelines on how to apply for a national visa for employment purposes, please click here (https://www.mzv.sk/en/web/dilli-en/visa-and-services/national-visa/employment).
Requests for booking appointments are sent to the email address - cons.riyadh@mzv.sk (mailto:cons.riyadh@mzv.sk).

Who may apply for a national visa
National (long-stay) visas are issued for stays of duration of more than 90 days. A third country national may be granted a national visa:
- On the basis of a granted residence permit.
- For the purpose of submitting a residence permit application in the Slovak Republic (employment, temporary employment, study, family reunification);
- If the applicant is older than 15 years and has been accepted for language education at a language school (List of Slovak language schools) with a minimum of 25 lessons per week;
- If it is in the interest of the Slovak Republic.
- If it is necessary in order to fulfill the obligations of the Slovak Republic following from international treaties.
- If the applicant is a family member of a foreigner who has been granted asylum or subsidiary protection.
For the table of recognized passports, visit the website of the Council of the European Union - Recognised travel documents (https://www.consilium.europa.eu/prado/en/prado-recognised-documents.html)
It is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for a foreigner who is also a citizen of the Slovak Republic
The Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh informs that it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for Slovak citizens in a travel document issued by a third country. Such visa application is not admissible.
If the child's parent is demonstrably a citizen of the Slovak Republic, it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for the child in a travel document issued by a third country.
A citizen of the Slovak Republic shall request the issuance of a passport or the issuance of a emergency travel document in order to travel to Slovak Republic. The emergency travel document is valid only for travel to Slovakia.
Please find more information at: Schengen Visa | Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For guidelines on how to apply for a national visa for employment purposes, please click here (https://www.mzv.sk/en/web/dilli-en/visa-and-services/national-visa/employment).
Requests for booking appointments are sent to the email address - cons.riyadh@mzv.sk (mailto:cons.riyadh@mzv.sk).

Who may apply for a national visa
National (long-stay) visas are issued for stays of duration of more than 90 days. A third country national may be granted a national visa:
- On the basis of a granted residence permit.
- For the purpose of submitting a residence permit application in the Slovak Republic (employment, temporary employment, study, family reunification);
- If the applicant is older than 15 years and has been accepted for language education at a language school (List of Slovak language schools) with a minimum of 25 lessons per week;
- If it is in the interest of the Slovak Republic.
- If it is necessary in order to fulfill the obligations of the Slovak Republic following from international treaties.
- If the applicant is a family member of a foreigner who has been granted asylum or subsidiary protection.
For the table of recognized passports, visit the website of the Council of the European Union - Recognised travel documents (https://www.consilium.europa.eu/prado/en/prado-recognised-documents.html)
It is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for a foreigner who is also a citizen of the Slovak Republic
The Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh informs that it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for Slovak citizens in a travel document issued by a third country. Such visa application is not admissible.
If the child's parent is demonstrably a citizen of the Slovak Republic, it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for the child in a travel document issued by a third country.
A citizen of the Slovak Republic shall request the issuance of a passport or the issuance of a emergency travel document in order to travel to Slovak Republic. The emergency travel document is valid only for travel to Slovakia.
Please find more information at: Schengen Visa | Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh 

## Visa Fees

[svk > sau > en > Table:Employment > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 156.40 SAR (Inclusive of 15% VAT) per application.
Please Note:
- The child between 0 - 12 years on the date of submission at VFS are Biometric exempt.
- The applicable visa fee in Saudi Riyals as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
This is an optional Value-added Service Available:

[svk > sau > en > Table: > Visa Fees 2]

## Documents Required
Checklist for TRP Employment
Checklist for TRP Family Reunification
Checklist for TRP Study
Checklist for Language Education
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries.
Please refer to the attached recognized list of Travel Medical Insurance Companies. 
The insured party, policy validity period, geographical coverage and schedule of benefits must be mentioned on the policy schedule/certificate.
Applicants are required to submit colored copy of passport and ID which shows clearly all information.
Translation of all Arabic documents into English or Slovak is mandatory.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 30 working days to process the visa application. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

## Download Forms
Download Application Form - English
Download Application Form - Slovak
This form must be completed when you are applying for a visa as a family
This form needs to be filled only when you are applying the visa for minor 
List of travel medical insurance companies – Click here  
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visit to family or friend visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for visiting your family member or friend.
General Public are hereby informed that family members of EU citizen are kindly requested to contact Embassy of Slovakia in, Riyadh pertaining to submission of their documents for visa.
It is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for a foreigner who is also a citizen of the Slovak Republic
The Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh informs that it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for Slovak citizens in a travel document issued by a third country. Such visa application is not admissible.
If the child's parent is demonstrably a citizen of the Slovak Republic, it is not possible to issue a Schengen visa for the child in a travel document issued by a third country.
A citizen of the Slovak Republic shall request the issuance of a passport or the issuance of a emergency travel document in order to travel to Slovak Republic. The emergency travel document is valid only for travel to Slovakia.
Please find more information at: Schengen Visa | Embassy of the Slovak Republic in Riyadh
